macro.intro: The macroeconomic landscape is dominated by rising inflation with CPI at 330.29, up from 327.46. The 10Y TIPS Real Yield has slightly decreased to 1.89%, indicating a potential shift in investor sentiment. The Fed Funds Rate remains steady at 3.64%, suggesting a cautious approach by the Federal Reserve. No major economic events are scheduled today, allowing markets to focus on geopolitical developments.

snapshot.name: WTI Crude Oil
snapshot.note: Volatility due to Iran export halt

snapshot.name: S&P 500
snapshot.note: Approaching all-time high, RSI nearing overbought

setups.name: Gold Long on Geopolitical Tensions
setups.rationale: Gold is gaining as a safe-haven asset amid the Iran crisis. With the CPI at 330.29, inflationary pressures support higher gold prices. The lack of major economic events today allows geopolitical factors to dominate. Entry at 1975.00, targeting 2020.00, with a stop at 1950.00.

metrics.label: CPI Inflation
metrics.text: High inflation supports gold as a hedge

metrics.label: 10Y TIPS Yield
metrics.text: Decline suggests lower real yields, boosting gold

metrics.label: Geopolitical Event
metrics.text: Iran export halt increases safe-haven demand

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: Resolution in US-Iran tensions could lower gold prices

setups.name: WTI Short on Iran Export Halt
setups.rationale: WTI Crude Oil is under pressure due to Iran halting petrochemical exports. The 10Y-2Y Yield Spread at 0.53% suggests economic caution. Entry at 83.00, targeting 78.00, with a stop at 85.00.

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: Resolution in US-Iran tensions could boost oil prices

setups.hedge: Consider long S&P 500 as a counter-cyclical hedge.

setups.name: S&P 500 Long on Risk-On Sentiment
setups.rationale: The S&P 500 is approaching its all-time high, supported by a risk-on sentiment as indicated by the VIX at 18.50. The Fed Funds Rate stability at 3.64% provides a supportive backdrop. Entry at 4500.00, targeting 4600.00, with a stop at 4450.00.

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: Geopolitical escalation could trigger a sell-off

contrarian.body: Despite the EUR/USD's recent rise, the pair faces strong resistance at 1.1000. With the Fed Funds Rate steady at 3.64%, the USD remains supported. Short at 1.0950, targeting 1.0850.
contrarian.warning: A breakthrough above 1.1000 could invalidate this setup. Monitor geopolitical developments closely as they could impact currency flows.

avoid.reason: Tech sector volatility due to geopolitical tensions and AI regulation news.
